prompt: This code throws NullPointerException. What is the bug?
choices: null strings must be converted with String.valueOf() before comparison
Calling .equals() on a null reference throws NullPointerException; compare as "test".equals(s) instead
== must be used when one operand may be null
String.equals() is not available for null arguments
explanation: s is null, so s.equals(...) dereferences a null pointer and throws NullPointerException. The fix is to flip the comparison: "test".equals(s). A string literal can never be null, so calling equals on it is safe. Alternatively, use Objects.equals(s, "test").
